description: A designed square promotional graphic for ICE's Student and Exchange Visitor Program (SEVP). The background is a moody, cinematic (likely AI-generated or heavily stylized) photo of a young person with a backpack seen from behind, silhouetted against a hazy sunset sky, gazing toward a billowing American flag on a pole at left. Overlaid in large white serif type at upper right is the headline 'Student and Exchange Visitor Program' (with 'and' smaller). Beneath it, smaller white sans-serif body text reads: 'Government agencies needing information about aliens coming to the U.S. primarily as students can contact us at:' followed by a blue angular call-to-action banner reading 'ICE.gov/SEVP' (with SEVP in orange). The lower right carries the circular blue DHS seal and the wordmark 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.' The patriotic flag imagery and 'aliens' terminology frame student-visa tracking as a national-security/surveillance function; the target audience is other government agencies and the general public.
notable_text: Student and Exchange Visitor Program; Government agencies needing information about aliens coming to the U.S. primarily as students can contact us at: ICE.gov/SEVP
